informal: Prove that $G$ cannot have a subgroup $H$ with $|H|=n-1$, where $n=|G|>2$.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_2_1_5 {G : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G]
  (hG : card G > 2) (H : Subgroup G) [Fintype H] :
  card H ≠ card G - 1
reasoning: The informal statement says: for a group G with |G| = n > 2, there is no subgroup H of order n-1. The Lean signature takes an arbitrary finite group G with card G > 2 and an arbitrary subgroup H, and concludes card H ≠ card G - 1, which is exactly the contrapositive-style formalization of "G cannot have a subgroup of order n-1". The Fintype assumption is implicit in the informal statement (n = |G| presupposes finiteness). Quantifier structure (∀ G, ∀ H) matches. No extra or missing hypotheses.
